name: SWEET-AND-SOUR SOUP WITH SEAWEED

ingredients.item: enoki mushrooms (see Note), rinsed with salted water and separated into bite-size bundles
fresh pineapple, cut into triangular slices
medium plum tomato, cut into wedges
tamarind paste or 1 teaspoon tamarind concentrate mixed with 2 teaspoons water
okras, sliced crosswise on the bias
dried seaweed (wakame or dulse; see Note), soaked and roughly chopped
silken tofu, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes
bird’s eye chili pepper, sliced, for garnish (optional)

method: In a large saucepan, heat oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until fragrant (about 1 minute). Add mushrooms, pineapple, tomato, and ½ teaspoon of the salt, and stir well.
Add tamarind paste and the water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Add okra, stock powder, remaining ½ teaspoon of salt, seaweed, and tofu, and bring to a boil again.
Add rice paddy herb and cilantro, and cook for 1 more minute only (overcooked seaweed tends to develop an overly fishy taste and lose its color and crunchiness). Remove from heat.
Garnish with chili slices (if using) and serve hot.
